# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official image to base our image on
FROM node:14-alpine3.12

# Set environment variable to handle build path
ENV BUILD_PATH /app

# Create app directory
RUN mkdir -p $BUILD_PATH

# Install build tools
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y \
        git \
        curl \
        wget \
        zip \
        unzip \
        tar \
        gzip \
        xz-utils \
        file

# Make sure the package index is up-to-date
RUN apt-get update

# Install Node.js packages for building
RUN apt-get install -y build-essential libssl-dev
RUN npm install --global yarn

# Set working directory to /app
WORKDIR $BUILD_PATH

# Copy application code into app directory
COPY . $BUILD_PATH

# Run command to build and start application
CMD ["npm", "run", "start"]
